topic: North Pole cosmic mountain — 6-text biblical convergence
change_summary: detail_evidence grew from 621 to 2129 chars (+243%); detail_verdict_text from 181 to 612 chars (+238%). Claim shortened from 91 to 26 chars. Content was substantially rewritten between c2 (2026-04-07) and now, implementing c2's recommendations (six-text naming, cross-cultural list, monitor.py argument).
our_argument_summary: WIN-031 is classified Unfalsifiable. The current detail_evidence is structured as three numbered problems: (1) retrodiction not prediction — texts predate the model; (2) non-discriminating archetype — axis mundi appears in Hindu/Norse/Greek/Islamic/Zoroastrian/Chinese cosmologies with mutually contradictory geometries; (3) the dome's own monitor.py (39 domains) excludes WIN-031, so the author's engineering implicitly agrees it is untestable. The theological layer is separated from the pole's geometric functions (r=0, H(r) apex, aetheric source), which are evaluated under other WINs.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN-031 listed as CONFIRMED, no criteria)
raw-text/05-model.txt ('North pole mountain (WIN-031)' listed in architecture)
raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (North Pole: Polaris 89.99, r=0, theta=-180.0 — geometric definition only)
data/sections.json part2b (documents monitor.py at commit 8e4bf9b with 39 domains)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-031 has NO calculation pipeline in any source we have examined. No domain name, no URL fetch, no comparison logic in the extracted raw-text. The CONFIRMED label is a static string on wins.html, 01-home.txt, and in the architecture paragraph of 05-model.txt. The pole's geometric role (r=0 in H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) km) is computed elsewhere for geometry WINs, but nothing computes or falsifies the theological cosmic-mountain claim.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded. The CONFIRMED status is a static string with no computation, no API, no measurement.
data_sources_used: None — WIN-031 uses no empirical data source
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None. However, the pole IS used as a physical parameter: r=0 in the H(r) formula (raw-text/05-model.txt), 'Polaris fixed above pole axis' for sidereal rotation, 'Axis Mundi — aetheric source/sink' in the toroidal flow description. These geometric roles are evaluated under separate WINs and are not in dispute for WIN-031.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: One caveat: our text names 'monitor.py, 39 domains' twice. The live poll data (chg-20260421-1011-002.json) reports 'passed=28/28' from status_history.json, not 39. Part 2b resolves this discrepancy by sourcing the 39 figure from a specific git commit (8e4bf9b) of monitor.py source. So the 39 is code-level (count of domain dictionaries defined in the .py file) while 28 is the runtime pass count reported to status_history.json. Our WIN-031 text does not clarify this distinction — a dome defender who checks the dome site's live numbers will find 28, not 39, and may use that to dismiss our claim.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain references WIN-031 in our extracted raw-text corpus or in part2b's census of monitor.py domains. The site's CONFIRMED label is a static string; no API fetch, no comparison, no falsification criterion.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: There is no standard-physics explanation of a 'cosmic mountain' archetype — it is a theological/mythological claim. Nothing is being relabeled.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The relevant comparison is retrodiction (texts predate the model), not post-hoc-in-the-adopted-observation sense. No published measurement preceded the dome's claim because there is no measurement to adopt.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The theological label is not derived from dome geometry. The pole's geometric roles (r=0, H(r) apex) are separate from the cosmic-mountain assertion, which is pure textual exegesis.

holes_found.description: The text promises 'six ancient texts' but names only five explicitly — Genesis 1:2, Job 37:18, Isaiah 40:22, Genesis 1:6–8, Hildegard of Bingen's Scivias — and fills the sixth slot with 'and related sources.' A dome defender will seize on this: 'They haven't even identified the six texts they are debunking.' This concedes ground on the numerical convergence claim and invites the rebuttal that we are arguing against a straw version of the dome's claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) investigate the dome site to pin down the sixth text — likely candidate is Deuteronomy 28:23 (cast-metal heaven, which WIN-034 cites) or Ezekiel 1's firmament vision; (b) re-word to 'a cluster of five named texts plus unspecified related sources' and flag that the dome never itemises the 'six' count. Either fix is acceptable; the current phrasing is the worst of both.

holes_found.description: TLDR/detail geographic inconsistency. The tldr_evidence lists the axis-mundi parallels as 'Hindu, Norse, Greek, Mesoamerican' — but the detail_evidence lists Hindu, Norse, Greek, Islamic, Zoroastrian, and Chinese. The TLDR names a tradition (Mesoamerican) the detail never substantiates, and drops three (Islamic, Zoroastrian, Chinese) the detail does substantiate. A reader who only skims the TLDR gets a different list of traditions than one who expands. This invites the pattern-matching critique: 'They are just rattling off mythologies.'
holes_found.recommendation: Align the two. Minimal fix: change tldr_evidence's 'Hindu, Norse, Greek, Mesoamerican' to 'Hindu, Norse, Greek, Chinese' (or another subset actually cited in the detail). Stronger fix: pick three canonical examples (e.g., Hindu/Mount Meru, Norse/Yggdrasil, Chinese/Kunlun) and use the same three in both TLDR and detail, then gesture at 'many others' in the detail paragraph.

holes_found.description: 'monitor.py, 39 domains' vs the dome site's live 'passed=28/28'. Our text twice cites 39 without sourcing. Part 2b grounds the figure in a specific git commit (8e4bf9b), but WIN-031's detail does not reference that. A dome defender checking the dome's current status page finds 28/28, not 39, and has grounds to dismiss the claim as wrong. The 39-vs-28 tension also weakens argument (3) — if the live monitor only scores 28 domains, the correct framing is 'the scorable-test set is 28 domains, and WIN-031 is not among them,' not 'the 39-domain monitor excludes WIN-031.'
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical citation to the 39 figure, e.g., 'monitor.py (39 domain dictionaries defined at commit 8e4bf9b; 28 currently score against status_history.json)' — or drop the number entirely and rely on the qualitative claim that no domain exists for WIN-031. The qualitative version is more defensible because it does not require the reader to trust a number that looks inconsistent with the live site.

holes_found.description: Lens A (advocate mode) surfaces a genuine rhetorical vulnerability the text does not preempt. A fair-minded dome defender can argue: 'Argument (3) is circular. Of course monitor.py does not test WIN-031 — monitor.py is for empirical/testable claims, and WIN-031 is classified by its author as a textual-exegesis confirmation. You are criticising the author for not empirically testing a claim whose genre the author would accept is non-empirical.' Our text should answer this: the reason monitor.py's exclusion matters is that the dome site nevertheless counts WIN-031 in its scorecard of 'confirmed predictions' and includes it in the 94.5% accuracy claim. That is the inconsistency we are exploiting — the author wants the rhetorical weight of scientific confirmation without submitting the claim to the author's own validation process.
holes_found.recommendation: Append a clarifying sentence at the end of argument (3): 'The point is not that every claim must be validated automatically; it is that WIN-031 is counted toward the dome's 94.5% confirmation scorecard alongside empirically tested claims like Schumann frequency or SAA field strength. The author wants it both ways — rhetorical weight of confirmed without submitting the claim to the author's own validation process.'

holes_found.description: TLDR says 'Six-text convergence is post-hoc.' Our tag sets post_hoc=false (the technical definition: a claim adopting a previously published empirical observation). The TLDR uses 'post-hoc' in its loose everyday sense (after the fact), which is fine for a non-technical summary but conflicts with the tag we publish in Part 2b. A careful reader cross-referencing the tag census against the TLDR will see an apparent contradiction.
holes_found.recommendation: Change the tldr_verdict phrase 'Six-text convergence is post-hoc' to 'Six-text convergence is retrodiction' — the detail already uses that term, and it matches the tag meaning without cost to TLDR accessibility.

holes_found.description: Argument (2) lists six parallels but calls only one of them ('Mount Qaf') by its mountain name on the Islamic side. Zoroastrian cosmology is named without its mountain (Harā Bərəz / Mount Hara, which the c2 deeper_analysis did cite). Small asymmetry, but critics who want to call the argument 'superficial' will notice.
holes_found.recommendation: Either name all mountains or none: 'Hindu (Mount Meru), Norse (Yggdrasil), Greek (Olympus), Islamic (Mount Qaf), Zoroastrian (Harā Bərəz), Chinese (Kunlun)' — one extra word per tradition for symmetry.

stronger_arguments.description: The structural argument we are not making explicitly: WIN-031 is counted in the denominator of the '69 confirmed / 94.5% accuracy' figure on the dome homepage and daily_review.json. If WIN-031 (and the other theological WINs 032, 034) are removed as unfalsifiable, the denominator drops and every other confirmation's weight increases proportionally. The real bite is that the theology-inclusive scorecard treats three unfalsifiable claims as indistinguishable from empirical ones. A dome reader who accepts our Unfalsifiable verdict for WIN-031 has a ready-made reason to discount the 94.5% number.

The dome's own page layering argues against WIN-031. raw-text/05-model.txt lists 'North pole mountain (WIN-031)' in the same architectural paragraph as 'Cast copper/bronze firmament (Job 37:18, Deut 28:23) — conductive metal ceiling creating resonant EM cavity' (WIN-034). The author deliberately grafts theology onto geometry: the pole gets r=0, the firmament gets Schumann resonance. For rhetorical force, we could point out that the author uses WIN-031 as scaffolding to make the copper-firmament claim feel 'scripturally grounded' — if WIN-031 evaporates as untestable, the scriptural-scaffold move applied elsewhere also weakens.

kernel_of_truth.description: The north pole IS a genuinely distinguished point: Earth's rotational axis (within ~10 km of geographic pole), the locus of Polaris overhead for northern observers, and the natural geometric origin of any axially symmetric model. Many unrelated traditions DID independently notice the pole's astronomical specialness (stellar permanence, circumpolar star tracks) and express it in sacred-center metaphors. The dome's choice of r=0 at the pole is geometrically natural for a disc topology, and the exponential H(r) centered at the pole is internally consistent. Cultural convergence on 'something special is at the pole' is not coincidence — it reflects real observational astronomy.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Observational specialness of the pole is not a 'confirmed prediction' of dome geometry — it is equally predicted by every axially symmetric cosmology that uses the rotational axis, including the standard spherical Earth (where the North Pole is also a locus of stellar permanence and navigational singularity). Textual agreement on a sacred-center archetype cannot discriminate between dome and sphere; both predict Polaris overhead at the pole and both are compatible with the axis-mundi motif. The claim is not that the dome gets the pole wrong — it is that the theological label adds no testable content beyond the geometric function, which is evaluated separately under WIN-029 and the H(r) formula WINs.

deeper_analysis:
WIN-031 has been substantially rewritten since the c2 review (2026-04-07). The c2 recommendations were largely implemented: the six texts are now named (though the sixth is vague), the cross-cultural counter-examples are listed, and the monitor.py exclusion argument is articulated as point (3). The verdict ('Unfalsifiable') holds. The structural arc — retrodiction, non-discriminating archetype, monitor.py exclusion — is sound and properly sequenced.

The three moderate holes are all polish issues, not conceptual errors. (a) The 'six texts' promise is not fulfilled with six named texts, inviting a dome-defender rebuttal. (b) The TLDR lists a different set of mythological parallels than the detail, creating an internal inconsistency for a skim reader. (c) The '39 domains' figure is defensible via Part 2b's sourcing to commit 8e4bf9b, but WIN-031's own text does not carry that sourcing, and the live dome site reports 28/28 passed (not 39), so the number looks wrong at first glance.

The advocate-mode lens surfaces the most interesting vulnerability: argument (3) is rhetorically circular if read charitably to the dome author. An untestable claim, by definition, will not appear in a test harness. The true force of argument (3) comes from the accounting: WIN-031 is counted in the '94.5% accuracy / 69 confirmed' scorecard even though it is structurally exempt from validation. That accounting critique is the load-bearing rhetorical beam — and the current text leaves it implicit.

Cross-WIN consistency is good for the theological cluster (WIN-031, 032, 034 all tagged Unfalsifiable, all share the retrodiction-vs-prediction critique). One adjacent concern: WIN-034 (firmament = copper/bronze) has verdict 'Unfalsifiable' but its own finding says 'copper dome would block all radio astronomy and contradict Schumann resonance' — that is an empirical, falsified, consequence, which argues for 'Refuted by Data' not 'Unfalsifiable.' This is a WIN-034 problem, not a WIN-031 problem, but if WIN-034 gets re-verdicted to 'Refuted by Data,' then WIN-031's neighbourhood argument ('the theological cluster is uniformly unfalsifiable') weakens — WIN-031 would stand alone as unfalsifiable while WIN-034 is refuted on physics grounds. The taxonomic point is: 'theological' and 'unfalsifiable' are not synonyms. We should not imply they are.

Quantitative verification: none needed — WIN-031 has no mathematical claim to verify.

citations_verified: raw-text/02-wins.txt line 116: WIN-031 — North Pole Cosmic Mountain — 6-Text Convergence CONFIRMED — verified
raw-text/05-model.txt: 'North pole mountain (WIN-031)' in architecture paragraph — verified
raw-text/01-home.txt: WIN-031 on confirmed list — verified
H(r) = 8537 x exp(-r / 8619) km — verified against raw-text/05-model.txt V12 KEY DISCOVERY section
data/sections.json part2b: 'monitor.py (commit 8e4bf9b)' sourcing for 39-domain figure — verified
citations_failed: The 'six ancient texts' list has only five named texts plus 'related sources'. The sixth is not cited. Our prose inherits this gap from the dome site (which also never enumerates six specific texts) but does not flag it.
The 39-domain count used twice in WIN-031's own detail is not sourced within WIN-031's text (it is sourced in Part 2b). Add parenthetical source.
citations_unchecked: Mircea Eliade citations ('The Sacred and the Profane', 'Myth and Reality') — general scholarly references, not contested, plausible.
The Zoroastrian parallel 'Harā Bərəz' was in the c2 review but the current detail only says 'Zoroastrian cosmology' without naming the mountain. Not wrong, just incomplete.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender who has read our methodological principles could argue: 'Your critique of WIN-031 relies on three moves, each of which has a charitable counter. (1) You call this retrodiction, but the dome model is explicitly a unified geometric-theological system; the cosmic-mountain archetype is not a post-hoc label on an empirical measurement, it is one of the SOURCES from which the geometric choice (r=0 apex) was derived. In your own framework of discriminatory power, the geometric-theological unification IS the discriminating feature — a pure-geometric pole-apex model would not need the label, while the dome's explicit scriptural derivation anchors the choice. (2) The cross-cultural axis mundi argument cuts both ways: if the archetype appears universally, that is Jungian / archetypal-psychology evidence that the pole is special in human cognition, which is more consistent with the dome than with an arbitrary spherical Earth where the pole is no more distinguished than any other point on the rotational axis. (3) The monitor.py exclusion argument is circular — the validation system is for empirical claims, and WIN-031 is classified as a textual-convergence claim. Your review does not dispute that convergence exists; you dispute only that the six texts pick the dome geometry uniquely. That is a disagreement about the rhetorical weight of the convergence, not about the convergence itself.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one preemptive sentence at the end of argument (3): 'The accounting is the point — WIN-031 counts toward the dome site 94.5% confirmation scorecard alongside empirically tested claims. We do not dispute textual convergence exists; we dispute that counting it toward a scorecard that includes tested physical predictions is honest accounting.' This neutralises the strongest dome-defender move (which is to accept our textual-convergence point and argue we are attacking a strawman of pure geometry).

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: The theological-cluster WINs (031, 032, 034) are all tagged Unfalsifiable, which reads as internally consistent. But WIN-034's own finding ('copper dome would block all radio astronomy and contradict Schumann resonance') is an empirical refutation, not an unfalsifiability claim — which means the cluster is not uniformly unfalsifiable: WIN-031 and WIN-032 are theology-only, while WIN-034 is theology-plus-empirical-consequence. A dome defender could compile: 'Your own review classifies WIN-034 as Unfalsifiable, but your own text within WIN-034 gives empirical grounds for a Refuted-by-Data verdict — which is it?' This is a WIN-034 taxonomy bug, but because WIN-031's argument implicitly leans on the theology-cluster uniformity, the bug echoes into WIN-031's rhetorical strength. Recommendation: raise this as an issue for WIN-034, not for WIN-031; leave WIN-031's verdict as-is.

claims_checked: No mathematical claims in WIN-031's text — verdict is Unfalsifiable on theological grounds with no numerical predictions.
quantitative_verification.computations: N/A — no formula, coupling constant, or measurement value to verify.
